title: senna cardiosperma subsp. manicula
intro:
Senna cardiosperma subsp. manicula, also known as the bush calycotome, is a plant species native to the southwestern region of Western Australia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and rounded leaflets.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include a high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides, responsible for its laxative effects. The health benefits of Senna cardiosperma subsp. manicula include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, relieve constipation, and support the treatment of digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used as a natural remedy for digestive health, typically in the form of dried leaves or tea infusions.
